Hume, gen'l sec'y. The Association's Reading Room has about 75 publications on file, including daily papers and the leading magazines, besides a library of about 500 volumes. The rooms are also provided with crockinole, chess, draughts, and other games for the amusement of the members, and a gymnasium well equipped for the use of the members. The quarterly business meeting of members is held on the second Friday evening in each month, and in connection
C. J. ALLARDT & CO'S with it a social and literary and musical entertainment is given.

The religious work of the Association consists of the Worker's Training Class for the training of Christian workers, which holds a meeting every Monday evening, and a gospel service for young men, and conducted by young men in the Parlor every Sunday afternoon.
The annual membership fee of the Association is two and dollars. There are about 135 members on the roll.
